---
title: "Enhance martix"
author: "MJY"
date: "2026-04-17"
output: html_document
---



################################################################################ 

# 

# OUTLINES:

# 1. Use HiC-Pro utilities to convert sparse Hi-C contact matrices into dense matrix format for downstream analysis.
# 2. Use awk to remove row and column headers from dense Hi-C matrices and generate plain numeric matrices for downstream analysis.
# 3. Use ScUnicorn to enhance Hi-C contact matrices and generate high-resolution interaction maps for downstream analysis.
# 4. Use Python to visualize enhanced Hi-C contact matrices and generate heatmaps for specific genomic regions.
# 

################################################################################ 

\#' \## <br> \#' 1. Use HiC-Pro utilities to convert sparse Hi-C contact matrices into dense matrix format for downstream analysis.


```{python eval=FALSE}

tad=/public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn
input=/public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/matrix
output=${tad}/01.DenseMatrix_iced
sample=(F1_F_CON_G1 F1_F_CON_G2)
soft=/home/WJlab/soft/HiC-Pro_3.1.0/bin/utils

for i in "${sample[@]}"
do
        date; echo
        echo "${i}_40000.iced.matrix begins converting to dense matrix."
        echo

        # Create output directory
        output_dir="${output}/${i}"
        mkdir -p "${output_dir}"
    
        # Enter the output directory
        cd "${output_dir}" || exit  # Exit if the directory does not exist or cannot be accessed

        # Run sparseToDense.py in the output directory
        python3 "${soft}/sparseToDense.py" "${input}/F1_F_CON/iced/40000/${i}_40000_iced.matrix" \
                                        -b "${input}/F1_F_CON/raw/40000/${i}_40000_abs.bed" \
                                        --perchr -i \
                                        -o "${i}_denseMatrix" > "${output}/${i}_40k.sparsetoDense.log" 2>&1

        # Return to the original directory
        cd - || exit

        echo
        date
        echo "${i} finished."
        echo

        # Ensure returning to the initial directory after each iteration
done

date
echo "All done."



```


\#' \## <br> \#' 2. Use awk to remove row and column headers from dense Hi-C matrices and generate plain numeric matrices for downstream analysis.


```{bash eval=FALSE}

# Process sample F1_F_CON_G1

# Use awk to remove the first row (column headers) and the first column (row names)
# Keep only the numeric matrix values for downstream analysis

awk 'NR>1{
    for(i=2;i<=NF;i++){
        printf $i" "
    }
    printf "\n"
}' /public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/01.DenseMatrix_iced/F1_F_CON_G1/chrX_F1_F_CON_G1_denseMatrix \
> /public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/01.DenseMatrix_iced/F1_F_CON_G1/chrX_F1_F_CON_G1_denseMatrix.unicorn.txt


# Process sample F1_F_CON_G2

# Use awk to remove the first row (column headers) and the first column (row names)
# Keep only the numeric matrix values for downstream analysis

awk 'NR>1{
    for(i=2;i<=NF;i++){
        printf $i" "
    }
    printf "\n"
}' /public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/01.DenseMatrix_iced/F1_F_CON_G2/chrX_F1_F_CON_G2_denseMatrix \
> /public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/01.DenseMatrix_iced/F1_F_CON_G2/chrX_F1_F_CON_G2_denseMatrix.unicorn.txt

```


\#' \## <br> \#' 3. Use ScUnicorn to enhance Hi-C contact matrices and generate high-resolution interaction maps for downstream analysis.

```{python eval=FALSE}

# Process sample F1_F_CON_G1

# Run ScUnicorn model to enhance Hi-C matrix resolution
# Input: dense matrix without headers
# Output: enhanced Hi-C matrix and visualization image

python3 /public/home/mjy/soft/Unicorn/Unicorn-main/ScUnicorn/scripts/generate_hr.py \
    --model_path /public/home/mjy/soft/Unicorn/Unicorn-main/ScUnicorn/checkpoint/scunicorn_model.pytorch \
    --data_path /public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/01.DenseMatrix_iced/F1_F_CON_G1/chrX_F1_F_CON_G1_denseMatrix.unicorn.txt \
    --output_image_path /public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/02.unicorn_enhance_iced/chrX_F1_F_CON_G1_denseMatrix.unicorn.txt.png \
    --output_hic_path /public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/02.unicorn_enhance_iced/chrX_F1_F_CON_G1_denseMatrix.unicorn.40kb.txt


# Process sample F1_F_CON_G2

# Run ScUnicorn model to enhance Hi-C matrix resolution
# Input: dense matrix without headers
# Output: enhanced Hi-C matrix and visualization image

python3 /public/home/mjy/soft/Unicorn/Unicorn-main/ScUnicorn/scripts/generate_hr.py \
    --model_path /public/home/mjy/soft/Unicorn/Unicorn-main/ScUnicorn/checkpoint/scunicorn_model.pytorch \
    --data_path /public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/01.DenseMatrix_iced/F1_F_CON_G2/chrX_F1_F_CON_G2_denseMatrix.unicorn.txt \
    --output_image_path /public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/02.unicorn_enhance_iced/chrX_F1_F_CON_G2_denseMatrix.unicorn.txt.png \
    --output_hic_path /public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/02.unicorn_enhance_iced/chrX_F1_F_CON_G2_denseMatrix.unicorn.40kb.txt


```

\#' \## <br> \#' 4. Use Python to visualize enhanced Hi-C contact matrices and generate heatmaps for specific genomic regions.

```{python eval=FALSE}

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Input file path (Unicorn-enhanced Hi-C matrix)
file_path = "/public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/02.unicorn_enhance_iced/chrX_F1_F_CON_G2_denseMatrix.unicorn.40kb.txt"

print("Loading matrix...")
m = np.loadtxt(file_path)

# Print matrix dimensions
print("Matrix shape:", m.shape)

# =========================
# Resolution setting (40 kb)
# =========================
bin_size = 40000

# Define genomic region (bp)
start = 75_000_000
end   = 95_000_000

# Convert genomic coordinates to bin indices
start_bin = start // bin_size
end_bin   = end   // bin_size

print("Bin range:", start_bin, end_bin)

# Extract submatrix corresponding to the region
sub = m[start_bin:end_bin, start_bin:end_bin]

# Log transformation for visualization
sub_log = np.log1p(sub)

# =========================
# Plot heatmap (Mb scale)
# =========================
extent = [start/1e6, end/1e6, start/1e6, end/1e6]

plt.figure(figsize=(6,6))
plt.imshow(sub_log, cmap="Reds", origin="lower", extent=extent)
plt.colorbar(label="log1p(contact count)")
plt.xlabel("Mb")
plt.ylabel("Mb")
plt.title("PWK chrX 75–95Mb (40kb, Unicorn)")

# Save figure
plt.savefig("/public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/02.unicorn_enhance_iced/G2_chrX_75_95Mb_40kb_unicorn.png", dpi=3000)
plt.close()

print("✅ chrX_75_95Mb_40kb_unicorn.png saved")


import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Input file path (Unicorn-enhanced Hi-C matrix)
file_path = "/public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/02.unicorn_enhance_iced/chrX_F1_F_CON_G1_denseMatrix.unicorn.40kb.txt"

print("Loading matrix...")
m = np.loadtxt(file_path)

# Print matrix dimensions
print("Matrix shape:", m.shape)

# =========================
# Resolution setting (40 kb)
# =========================
bin_size = 40000

# Define genomic region (bp)
start = 75_000_000
end   = 95_000_000

# Convert genomic coordinates to bin indices
start_bin = start // bin_size
end_bin   = end   // bin_size

print("Bin range:", start_bin, end_bin)

# Extract submatrix corresponding to the region
sub = m[start_bin:end_bin, start_bin:end_bin]

# Log transformation for visualization
sub_log = np.log1p(sub)

# =========================
# Plot heatmap (Mb scale)
# =========================
extent = [start/1e6, end/1e6, start/1e6, end/1e6]

plt.figure(figsize=(6,6))
plt.imshow(sub_log, cmap="Reds", origin="lower", extent=extent)
plt.colorbar(label="log1p(contact count)")
plt.xlabel("Mb")
plt.ylabel("Mb")
plt.title("C57 chrX 75–95Mb (40kb, Unicorn)")

# Save figure
plt.savefig("/public/home/mjy/HCC/F1/HiC/F1_allvaildpairs/Unicorn/02.unicorn_enhance_iced/G1_chrX_75_95Mb_40kb_unicorn.png", dpi=3000)
plt.close()

print("✅ chrX_75_95Mb_40kb_unicorn.png saved")


```
